title: Explicit heat-equation update is monotone under the parabolic CFL condition

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: If 0 <= r and 2r <= 1, then increasing each of the left, center, and right scalar inputs cannot decrease the explicit heat update.
informal.latex: If \(0\le r\), \(2r\le1\), and \(u_L\le v_L\), \(u\le v\), \(u_R\le v_R\), then \({\mathcal H}_r(u_L,u,u_R)\le {\mathcal H}_r(v_L,v,v_R)\).
assumptions: r, uLeft, u, uRight, vLeft, v, and vRight are real numbers
informal.expected_conclusion: The scalar heat update is monotone in all three state arguments.

declarations.name: heatStep
declarations.statement: def heatStep (r uLeft u uRight : Real) : Real := heatLeftWeight r * uLeft + heatCenterWeight r * u + heatRightWeight r * uRight

declarations.name: heatStep_monotone
declarations.statement: theorem heatStep_monotone (r uLeft u uRight vLeft v vRight : Real) (hr0 : 0 <= r) (hrCfl : 2 * r <= 1) (hLeft : uLeft <= vLeft) (h : u <= v) (hRight : uRight <= vRight) : heatStep r uLeft u uRight <= heatStep r vLeft v vRight

tasks.input: Translate this explicit heat-equation finite-difference theorem into Lean 4: If 0 <= r and 2r <= 1, then increasing each of the left, center, and right scalar inputs cannot decrease the explicit heat update.
tasks.target: theorem heatStep_monotone (r uLeft u uRight vLeft v vRight : Real) (hr0 : 0 <= r) (hrCfl : 2 * r <= 1) (hLeft : uLeft <= vLeft) (h : u <= v) (hRight : uRight <= vRight) : heatStep r uLeft u uRight <= heatStep r vLeft v vRight

tasks.input: Given the heat-equation scaffold definitions and theorem statement, complete the Lean 4 proof.
tasks.target: A proof using heatWeights_nonneg, mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left, and linarith.

tasks.input: Find the LeanNumerics lemma that supplies nonnegative heat-equation weights under the CFL assumptions.
